verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is canonically the beta subunit of mitochondrial F1Fo ATP synthase in the inner mitochondrial membrane matrix-facing complex. However, ectopic surface expression of ATP synthase components (including the beta subunit) on the plasma membrane of tumor cells, hepatocytes, endothelial cells, and adipocytes is well documented — 'ecto-F1-ATPase' / 'ecto-ATP synthase' acts as a receptor for apoA-I, angiostatin, and other ligands, and is being explored as a tumor surface target. This non-baseline surface display on transformed and selected normal cells maps to cell_state_induced rather than no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether ecto-ATP synthase surface display is robust/specific enough on tumor cells for therapeutic targeting vs. an artifact-prone minor pool.
